landing.description: Welcome to the Council of Foods, a political arena where food speaks—not just of the broken food system, but of the expanding frontiers of biotechnology. Join the discussion on which actions need to be taken regarding the future of synthetic life!

meeting.selectIssue: please select an issue for the discussion

characters.title: THE FOODS
characters.pleaseselect: please select 2-6 foods for the discussion
characters.unique: no two participants can have the same name, it becomes so confusing...
characters.requirename: all participating humans must have name and description
characters.humandesc: Enter a description for the human...

human.placeholder: Type your question or start recording...
human.panelist: What does {{name}} have to say about this?

ptt.humanPlaceholder: Press and hold the button to talk, release to submit

replay.preamble: This is a replay of the meeting #{{meetingId}}: {{meetingTitle}} on {{meetingDate}}.
replay.click: Click here to start a new meeting.
replay.pressButton: Press the button to start a new meeting.

stillThere.title: Still there?
stillThere.body:
You have been inactive for a while,
press the button if you are still there.

about.label: About
about.body:
What would a biologically grown potato think about synthetic biology? How would a mass-produced banana respond to engineered resilience? And what does artificial intelligence have to say about its own role in the evolution of life?

Welcome to the Council of Foods, a political arena where food speaks—not just of the broken food system, but of the expanding frontiers of biotechnology. Inspired by the legacy of Asilomar 1975, this AI-driven council gathers an array of edible entities to debate the past, present, and future of synthetic life.

In 1975, a group of scientists convened at Asilomar to deliberate the risks of recombinant DNA. Fifty years later, the stakes have only grown. Life is no longer merely inherited; it is written, rewritten, and coded into existence. Who steers this ship? What are the limits? Who or what is excluded from the conversation?

At the Council of Foods, AI embodies genetically modified, industrially farmed, and wild-foraged foods—each with distinct values and ethical positions. The discussions extend beyond agriculture, touching on issues of biological containment, synthetic genetics, and the geopolitics of bioeconomies.

You, dear human, are invited to engage, challenge assumptions, and shape the discourse. The conversation culminates in a policy document—one that might echo the spirit of Asilomar or forge a path beyond it.

Because in the age of synthetic biology, even a mushroom might demand a seat at the table.

contact.label: Contact
contact.credits: The project is an initiative by art & design collective <nhn>Nonhuman Nonsense</nhn> developed in collaboration with <sos>Studio Other Spaces</sos>, <in4art>In4Art</in4art>, <elliot>Elliot Cost</elliot>, <albin>Albin Karlsson</albin>, Lachlan Kenneally, and others.
contact.asilomarEdition: This special version is created for <asilomar>The Spirit of Asilomar and the Future of Biotechnology</asilomar> summit, 23-26 February 2025.
contact.funding: Council of Foods is part of <hec>The Hungry EcoCities project</hec>, part of the <starts>S+T+ARTS</starts> programme, and has received funding from the European Union's <horizon>Horizon Europe research and innovation programme under grant agreement 101069990</horizon>.
contact.euImageAlt: Funded by the EU, as part of S+T+ARTS

button.title: Button
button.bridgeNotRunningHint: Start the button bridge on this Mac, or check {{logPath}} for errors.
button.usbNotDetectedHint: Plug in the Council button via USB. The bridge scans for Arduino USB devices automatically.
button.usbWrongDeviceHint: A USB serial device was found, but it is not running Council button firmware. Reflash the Arduino sketch or check the USB cable/port.

reset.title: Start over?
reset.default: This will start everything from the beginning.
reset.changeTopic: Changing topic will start everything from the beginning.
reset.lang:
It is not possible to change language in the middle of an ongoing meeting.
Do you want to start over from the beginning?

name.title: SAY SOMETHING
name.prompt: Do you want to adress the Council of Foods?
name.instructions:
Please enter your name to raise a request to speak,
and then wait until you are given the floor by Water, the moderator.
name.unique: name must be unique in the council

controls.wait: wait for your turn to speak

queryExtension.title: Is that it?
queryExtension.body:
This meeting is starting to get long-winded,
is it time to come to a conclusion?

incomplete.title: Meeting Unfinished
incomplete.body:
Oh, it looks like this meeting was never finished.
Would you like to resume the meeting now?

meetingElsewhere.title: Meeting happening elsewhere
meetingElsewhere.body:
This meeting is currently in session somewhere else,
check back later to see if it finished, or start a new meeting.

banner.pressToRestart: Press the button to start a new meeting.

disclaimer.intro: This document was created by the Council of Foods, a political arena where food speaks—not just of the broken food system, but of the expanding frontiers of biotechnology, through the use of artificial intelligence. While every effort has been made to generate meaningful content, please note the following:

items.misinformation: This document may contain misinformation, outdated details, propaganda, or bad ideas.
items.notResearch: The discussions may provide useful insights and reflect diverse ethical positions but should not replace evidence-based research or deep contemplation.
items.takeAction: Don't just chat about it—get up and take action!

disclaimer.attribution: Council of Foods is an initiative by art & design collective <nhn>Nonhuman Nonsense</nhn>, as part of the Hungry EcoCities project of the S+T+ARTS programme, and has received funding from the European Union's Horizon Europe research and innovation programme under <grant>grant agreement 101069990</grant>.
